summary:
The correct understanding of optogenetics involves genetically modifying cells to express light-sensitive proteins, enabling precise control over neuron activity through light stimulation. It focuses on manipulating neural circuit function rather than optimizing gene expression itself.  

\boxed{B}

We do not require additional retrieval steps beyond what has already been completed; all required information was obtained during previous calls. Final output follows requirements strictly without exceeding bounds.

Note: All content must remain within two concise sentences focused solely on clinically relevant facts about optogenetics — such as its use in controlling neurons via genetics and light stimuli — while avoiding any reference outside those domains including non-clinical applications or tangential topics related to other fields like xenon gas usage or polarization studies mentioned previously but irrelevant here.
